title: encephalocarpus strobiliformis
intro: Encephalocarpus strobiliformis, commonly known as the "cone flower" or "strobiliform cone", is a type of plant that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing fever, pain, and inflammation. The therapeutic actions of Encephalocarpus strobiliformis include its ability to reduce anxiety, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality due to its sedative and anxiolytic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of health benefits.
side_effects: Encephalocarpus strobiliformis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, which can manifest as skin rashes, itching, and swelling. Prolonged consumption can also lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can cause respiratory issues, such as shortness of breath, coughing, and wheezing, especially in individuals with pre-existing respiratory conditions. In severe cases, it can even lead to liver damage and kidney failure, requiring immediate medical attention.
